titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Doris Bry Papers
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke Staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0., has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 8894126.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Tuesday December 29, 2020 at 04:00
Finding aid updated with January 2017 acquisition.

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.physdesc: (304 boxes) + 15 art, 2 rolls, 16 broadsides, 1 oversize broadside

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: January 2017 acquisition: Gift of the Doris Bry Trust, 2017.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, and competing priorities. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
The collection is comprised of material formerly classed as: Uncat MSS 433, Uncat MSS 635, Uncat MSS 1027.

The collection received preliminary processing at the time of acquisition. Further rehousing, and description were carried out in 2014. Various acquisitions associated with the collection have not been merged and organized as a whole. Each acquisition is described separately in the contents list below, titled according to month and year of acquisition.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.

Researchers should note that material within each acquisition overlaps with and/or relates to material found in other acquisitions. In order to locate all relevant material within this collection, researchers will need to consult each acquisition described in the Collection Contents section. Researchers should also note that similar material can be arranged differently in each acquisition, depending on how the material was organized when it was received by the library.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

bioghist.head: Doris Bry
bioghist.p: Doris Bry (1920-2014) was a scholar of Alfred Stieglitz, and was Georgia O'Keeffe's business agent and confidant. She is the author of Alfred Stieglietz: Photographer (1965), and editor or co-editor of Some Memories of Drawings (by Georgia O'Keeffe, 1974) , Georgia O'Keeffe, in the West (1989), and Georgia O'Keeffe: The New York Years (1991).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection consists of correspondence, publication material, art work, printed material, photographs, and other papers relating to Doris Bry's various editorial projects and professional association with Georgia O'Keeffe. The January 2017 acquisition consists of records organized by and received from the Doris Bry Trust, including correspondence between Bry and O'Keeffe, Alfred Stieglitz estate papers, research materials for catalogues raissoné for O'Keeffe and Stieglitz, records of Bry's work as an art dealer, legal papers, publishing projects, additional correspondence and personal papers, audiovisual materials, and computer media.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The collection is organized into four groupings: November 2002 acquisition; February 2004 acquisition; June 2008 acquisition; and January 2917 acquisition.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.
13 (Art)-14 (Art), Boxes 15 (Oversize)-17 (Oversize), 18 (Art)-20 (Art) and 21 (Roll): Restricted material. For further information consult the appropriate curator.
Boxes 53-120, 134-191, 207, 268-270, 280-282, 302, 306-309, 316, 319-320: Restricted until December 5, 2060. For further information consult the appropriate curator.
Boxes 121-133, 255-258, 305: Restricted until December 5, 2040. For further information consult the appropriate curator.
Boxes 261, 264, 305, 315, and 316 (born digital): Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Boxes 97, 175, 259, 260, 262, 263, 304, and 314 (audiovisual material): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Consists of publication materials, some related to Georgia O'Keeffe. Includes works by Richard Benson and others, magazines, sample book covers, Artforum magazine, mechanicals for Some Memories of Drawings , Irving Penn proofs of Mud Glove , and other related papers

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: 13 (Art)-14 (Art), Boxes 15 (Oversize)-17 (Oversize), 18 (Art)-20 (Art) and 21 (Roll): Restricted material. For further information consult the appropriate curator.

did.unittitle: "O'Keeffe''s work with [Bry] on layout of Whitney book 1970, 1 cover"
"Folder from Bert Clark re: Whitney book, 1970"
Tearsheets from March 1, 1968 Life magazine O'Keeffe article
Book cover samples for Georgia O'Keeffe
Book cover sample bound with text block for Georgia O'Keeffe
Cover spine samples for Georgia O'Keeffe
Portfolio box cover samples for Georgia O'Keeffe Drawings
"Amon Carter cover proof -1966 Texas show - Winter Road"
Production materials for Georgia O'Keeffe Drawings
"Layout for prospectus of 1988 trade edition of Some Memories of Drawings from Bert Clarke "
"Mechanicals, Some Memories of Drawings , trade edition, 1988"
Sample cases for Some Memories of Drawings by Georgia O'Keeffe
"The M. Carey Thomas Awards," Bryn Mawr College (3 copies)
Santa Fe Chamber Music Festival, Season 3, 4, and 5, three posters, signed; "Sky Above the Clouds," by Georgia O'Keeffe, reproduction, third proof

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged into four groupings: Correspondence, Gifts by Artists, Printed Material, and Georgia O'Keeffe Prints (Framed)

did.unittitle: Doris Bry and Barbara Lynes correspondence

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a photogravure proof by Richard Benson for Eakins Press, from the Beinecke Library's Carl Van Vechten negative.

did.unittitle: Binder used by Alfred Stieglitz for "Camera Work"
Clarke, Bert - layout and design of tray cases for "Camera Work"
Evans, Frederich H. - "Turn-Elliptic Pendulum Cruves" (gift of Sam Wagstaff)
Girard, Alexander - "Georgia O'Keeffe," photograph (sent to Doris Bry by Georgia O'Keeffe in July 1970)
"Honk if you Love Stieglitz," auto sticker
Jacobi, Lotte - "Josef Scharl," photographs (purchased by Doris Bry from the estate of Lotte Jacobi)
KQED * 9, Georgia O'Keeffe, photographs by Jim Farber, San Francisco
Meriden Gravure Co. proof - "Walker Evans: Forty Years"
Scheinbaum & Russek Ltd. - three Christmas cards and photographs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes invitations for O'Keeffe retrospective exhibitions, 1946 and 1970/1971, and an Alfred Stieglitz exhibtion at the Museum of Modern Art, 1947.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes pamphlets, programs, gallery guides, catalogues, and newspapers about artists and photographers.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of the Doris Bry Trust, 2017.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The Doris Bry Trust organized their records into fourteen series: I. Doris Bry and Georgia O'Keeffe correspondence. II. Materials relating to the Alfred Stieglitz estate. III. Bry Catalogue Raisonné materials for Alfred Stieglitz photographs. IV. Bry Catalogue Raisonné materials for Georgia O'Keeffe. V. Bry records and correspondence related to work as an art dealer. VI. Legal files. VII. Materials related to conservation. VIII. Permissions. IX. Bry publishin projects and consultations. X. General correspondence. XI. Additional business amterial. XII. Tapes and cassettes. XIII. Doris Bry personal papers. XIV. Other papers.
This acquisition maintains that organization and numbering. Series VII. Materials related to conservation and Series VIII. Permissions were not received and are not included in this acquisition.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Boxes 53-120, 134-191, 207, 268-270, 280-282, 302, 306-309, 316, 319-320: Restricted until December 5, 2060. For further information consult the appropriate curator.
Boxes 121-133, 255-258, 305: Restricted until December 5, 2040. For further information consult the appropriate curator.
Boxes 261, 264, 305, 315, and 316 (born digital): Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Boxes 97, 175, 259, 260, 262, 263, 304, and 314 (audiovisual material): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The January 2017 acquisition consists of records organized by and received from the Doris Bry Trust, including correspondence between Bry and O'Keeffe, Alfred Stieglitz estate papers, research materials for catalogues raissoné for O'Keeffe and Stieglitz, records of Bry's work as an art dealer, legal papers, publishing projects, additional correspondence and personal papers, audiovisual materials, and computer media.

did.unittitle: Doris Bry and Georgia O'Keeffe correspondence

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This series is organized into three subseries: a. Bry to O'Keeffe, b. O'Keeffe to Bry, and c. Related correspondence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence with Frances O'Brien, Edith Halpert, and others.

arrangement.p: This series is organized into three subseries: a. Stieglitz research notes, b. Stieglitz visual material, and c. Stieglitz index cards.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This series is organized into four subseries: a. O'Keeffe v. Bry files, b. Bry v. Hamilton files, c. Mixed files for both actions, and d. O'Keeffe family v. Hamilton.
The Doris Bry Trust's original arrangement of this series included a fifth subseries, e. O'Keeffe financial records, but those records were not received.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains legal correspondence and transcripts of Doris Bry depositions.

did.unittitle: Both actions--O'Keeffe vs. Bry and Bry vs. Hamilton
Both actions--O'Keeffe vs. Bry and Bry vs. Hamilton

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This series is organized into four subseries: a. Atlantis Editions: O'Keeffe Drawing Portfolio / Some Memories of Drawings / Posters, b. Work on catalogues / books / printing, c. Callaway editions (consulting), and e. Material for "World of O'Keeffe".
The Doris Bry Trust's original arrangement of this series included subseries d. Notes for Bry "Computer" book, but those records were not received.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This series is organized into six subseries: a. Doris Bry photography, b. Family correspondence, photographs, and mementos, c. Personalized books, d. Miscellaneous personal artifacts, e. Estate planning, and f. Tax files.
